title: rosa moschata var. helenae
intro: Rosa moschata var. helenae, also known as Helen's musk rose, is a species of rose plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is valued for its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is rich in antioxidants and contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against infections. Rosa moschata var. helenae can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in perfumery and as a decorative plant in gardens.
